description: Persistent issue tracker. Decider appends new issues from Curmudgeon reviews, marks resolved when wins.json no longer contains the flagged text.

issues.description: VERDICT CATEGORY OVERLOAD: 'Misleading' verdict conflates 4 distinct failure modes: (1) data misrepresentation, (2) duplication/redundancy, (3) non-discrimination (observation explained by multiple models equally), (4) circular derivation. 23 WINs under one label implies systematic dishonesty, but many are simply non-discriminating observations. Consider subcategory 'Non-Discriminating' or move non-discriminating WINs to 'Std Model Explains'. Affects verdict taxonomy coherence and reviewer's interpretation of dome author intent.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Verdict taxonomy restructuring requires human editorial decision on subcategory definitions

issues.description: Refractive index discrepancy: dome model page explicitly shows n(r) formula yielding n=3.49 at ice wall (20,015 km). Our review cites 28.8. Flagged 2 CONSECUTIVE polls. Either: (a) our formula extrapolates beyond the ice wall, (b) dome revised n(r), or (c) we use a different formula. Analyst must reconcile — if we have the wrong number, this is a factual error in our review. Check raw-text/ for dome formula vs our derivation.
issues.notes: Yeeted to analyst as EXP-050 for n(r) formula reconciliation. [2026-04-09] 4th consecutive poll flag. Resolution flows through CAT-005/EXP-050 curmudgeon review (ISS-696 blocked-on-curmudgeon). After curmudgeon review, analyst will write actual section prose reconciling the 28.8 vs 3.49 discrepancy.
issues.poll_note: 4th consecutive poll (2026-04-09 21:44Z). Poller continues to flag n(r) discrepancy: dome model page = 3.49 at ice wall, our review = 28.8. CAT-005/EXP-050 proposal package for Refraction Cascade is now queued for curmudgeon review (blocked-on-curmudgeon). Resolution will flow through that channel.

issues.description: W048 confidence downgrade: dome self-corrected WIN-048 (CMB axis of evil) from ">99.9% confirmation" to "2–3σ" after Planck full-mission review. This is a responsible correction our review should acknowledge. Check if our WIN-048 detail text overstates the original claim. Flagged by poller.

GitHub PAT stored on disk in workspace FUSE mount at .workspace-sync-token (gitignored, chmod 600). Required so the dome-workspace-sync agent — which runs in its own ephemeral session at a different path — can authenticate pushes without depending on dome-review-clean existing in its session. File is local-only (never committed, verified via git check-ignore and git ls-files), so threat surface is limited to processes running as the workspace user on this machine. Cleanup options to consider when convenient: (a) replace static PAT with a short-lived GitHub App installation token minted on demand, (b) move secret to an OS keychain / credential helper that the agent can call into instead of a flat file, (c) at minimum, rotate the PAT periodically and document the rotation procedure (update .workspace-sync-token AND dome-review-clean/.git/config remote URL, then revoke old PAT). Not urgent — file is not exposed beyond the local VM workspace.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Secure credential storage requires human decision on approach (GitHub App vs keychain vs rotation policy)

issues.description: The analyst pipeline does not currently form views on the dome's 10 Prospective Wins (PROS-001..PROS-008 plus WIN-057, WIN-058 which sit in both buckets). Mode 0 (new WIN onboarding) only triggers when the dome's confirmed WIN count exceeds ours, so prospective predictions are invisible to the review pipeline. CONTEXT: The PROS bucket is where the dome stages predictions before promoting them to confirmed wins. Tracking them would let us (a) pre-empt his promotion narrative — when he announces "PROS-001 confirmed, now WIN-070", we already have a critique ready; (b) catch silent suspensions at the prospective level (PROS-003 went SUSPENDED in the 2026-03-15 batch alongside WIN-010/WIN-025 and we missed it until today); (c) score his prospective hit rate over time as a separate accuracy figure that is actually predictive rather than retrodictive. PROPOSAL: Add a new analyst Mode 5 (prospective tracking) and a new data file data/prospective.json with one entry per PROS item. Each entry would capture: prediction text, stated test conditions, our pre-emptive critique, current dome status (active / promoted / suspended / removed), and link to the WIN entry if/when promoted. The poller already fetches the prospective section, so the data is available; just needs a Mode 5 to act on it. The new headline structure on the wins tab could grow a fifth metric for prospective hit rate once Mode 5 has produced enough data. DESIGN DECISIONS for analyst: should prospective items live in a separate file or as a section of wins.json? Should there be a new tab on the review site, or just additional rows in the WINs tab? Should the silent-failures counter pull in suspended-prospective items (currently FAIL-008 is the only one, manually backfilled today)?
issues.source: human discussion 2026-04-09 — surfaced during silent-failures counter design
issues.rationale_for_deferral: New analyst mode design and data file structure decision — needs analyst input rather than ad-hoc human implementation.

issues.description: Formally rate kill-shot items using the same verdict taxonomy and analytical fields as WINs. CORE PROBLEM: Our current kill-shot presentation buries the verdict inside prose. A reader has to parse a full paragraph to figure out whether our conclusion on a given kill shot is 'Std Model Explains', 'Self-Contradicted', 'Refuted by Data', etc. The verdict is the tl;dr — it should be visible as a badge, not extracted by close reading. Kill shots currently live in their own tab (since V6.0 restructure) and are presented as curated highlights rather than as individually scored entries. They lack the structured verdict (Refuted by Data / Self-Contradicted / Std Model Explains / Misleading / Not Demonstrated / Unfalsifiable), the code_analysis tags (monitoring / relabels_standard / post_hoc / derives_from_dome), and the other data fields that give WINs a consistent analytical spine. The curmudgeon's per-item review lifecycle also does not apply to them uniformly. TASK: (1) Identify the canonical kill-shot list and current data shape — are they a subset of wins.json, a separate file, or inlined in sections.json? (2) Propose a schema that brings them to parity with WINs: at minimum verdict, finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and code_analysis tags. They probably do not need new_in_v51 or detail_group. (3) Apply the schema, assigning a verdict to each kill shot by reading the existing prose and extracting the implicit verdict. Expect a mix — some will be Self-Contradicted or Refuted by Data, but others will land on 'Std Model Explains' (where our point is that the dome's claim adds nothing beyond standard physics) or 'Misleading' (cherry-picked / circular). Do NOT assume all kill shots are Self-Contradicted just because they are the strongest arguments. NEW VERDICT CATEGORY — 'Wait and See' (a.k.a. Legit Pending): introduce this as a valid verdict ONLY in the kill-shot scope for now. It applies when the dome has put forward a proposal that is (a) genuinely unique — not a relabel of standard physics, not already measured — (b) grounded in data the dome has actually produced or can point to, and (c) verifiable in principle, just waiting on time or observation to resolve. This is the honest 'we can't refute this yet, it's a real open prediction' bucket. Important: a Wait-and-See verdict is a compliment — it means the dome has stated something falsifiable and non-trivial. Expect this to be rare; most kill shots will still land in the critical categories. The analyst should be strict about the three criteria — if any one fails, fall back to 'Not Demonstrated' or the appropriate critical verdict. This category will also carry over to ISS-677's Prospective Wins work when that lands, but for now scope it to kill shots only so we don't confuse the headline tallies. Make sure the color/badge for Wait-and-See is visually distinct from the six existing critical verdicts — probably a neutral blue or grey, not the green of 'Std Model Explains'. (4) Update the kill shots tab render to display the verdict badge prominently next to each item, using the same color scheme as the WINs tab. (5) Once tagged, kill shots should enter the curmudgeon review lifecycle so they get adversarial scrutiny on the same cadence. EXPLICITLY NOT IN SCOPE: Kill-shot verdicts should NOT count into the top-line hero tallies or the 4-card scorecard. A kill shot is a curated highlight of an existing argument; double-counting it in the verdict tallies would inflate the numbers. The verdict badge is for reader comprehension, not for aggregation. The existing Kill Shots tab badge is sufficient — no fifth hero card needed. DESIGN QUESTIONS for analyst: Should kill shots remain a separate tab or become a filtered view of WINs with a kill_shot:true flag? If the latter, how do we prevent the filtered view from double-counting in the WINs tab tallies? PRIORITY: Above baseline curmudgeon re-reviews but below ISS-676 (V50.6→V51.1 reconciliation) and ISS-677 (Prospective Wins Mode 5). The reconciliation work may change which kill shots are even valid, so this is better sequenced after ISS-676.
issues.rationale_for_deferral: Schema design and re-categorization work best handled by analyst with full context; not urgent, but should not wait indefinitely behind baseline review cycles.

issues.description: HOLE-067-C2-004: Ebbing et al. 2021 JGR citation uses DOI 10.1029/2019JB018855. AGU DOI encodes submission year (2019). Verify DOI resolves to: (a) a paper by Ebbing about East Antarctic mantle/gravity anomaly, (b) published year 2021. If not 2021, replace with verified citation like Pappa et al. 2019 or Ferraccioli et al. 2011.

9 workspace-only analyst files not committed to git, at risk of loss if FUSE mount is reset. Files: GLOBE-AUDIT-EXECUTIVE-SUMMARY.txt, globe-dependencies-inventory.txt, gravity-dependency-map.json, origin-theory.md, python-files-inventory.txt, v51-1-cascade-analysis.json, wgs84-dependency-audit.json, wgs84-dependency-audit.md, wgs84-detailed-findings.txt. workspace-sync agent should commit these.
issues.notes: [2026-04-09] workspace-sync agent should commit these files. Decider cannot commit workspace-only files directly (FUSE filesystem ownership rules).

issues.description: Claim drift: dome site marks WIN-025 (2024 Eclipse 9-Station Confirmation) as [REMOVED] due to storm contamination. Our review still presents it as a current claim. Needs annotation noting dome removed this WIN, or restructuring to make this clear. WIN-025 is in uncounted-failures already; review text should acknowledge removal.

CAT-005 proposal: Analyst proposes new section "V51.1 Refraction Cascade: How One Parameter Fix Breaks Everything" covering 12+ affected WINs. Cascade failure analysis shows refraction needed is 13-27 degrees vs 0.03 degrees real. Human must decide: approve as new section (Part 2 or Section 7 extension), reject, or integrate as individual WIN patches.
issues.human_decision: Approved as new section under The Model tab (option a). New code_analysis sub-category tag requested for cascade-affected WINs. Analyst to write recommended proposal package — route to curmudgeon before fleshing out prose. See CAT-005.json human_decision block.
issues.blocked_reason: awaiting curmudgeon review of EXP-050 CAT-005 V51.1 Refraction Cascade proposal package

issues.description: External report #1 (Devilwench): Positive feedback only. No error claim, correction, or scientific content. "You're awesome don't ever change." No changes needed.
issues.fix_details: Not a problem report — positive feedback only. Analyst logged as reader engagement data point.

issues.description: Social rebrand keywords meta tag expansion: social analyst recommends adding rebrand-resistant keywords to HTML meta keywords tag. Current: ovoid cavity cosmological model, dome model, ECM, firmament resonance, aetheric medium. Recommended additions: Dome Cosmology, Aetheric Dome Model, Firmament Cavity Model (top 3 from rebrand-resistance-strategy.md). Action: human or decider should patch generate-html.js meta keywords string. Ref: monitor/social/drafts/rebrand-resistance-strategy.md
